commit.message:
Per-strategy V2 IP thresholds: N=201, PF=2.25, +81.6R

Each strategy now gets its own optimal IP threshold instead of one
global value. Thresholds set from per-strategy sweep data:

  HH_QUALITY:     0.79  (PF peaks here, dips at 0.75)
  EMA_FPIP_V3_B:  0.73  (PF rises as threshold drops, +51R)
  SP_V2_SIMPLE:   0.77  (PF peaks at 0.77, +34.7R)
  ORH_FBO_V2_B:   0.80  (elite sleeve, keep tight)
  ORH_FBO_V2_A:   0.78  (N grows without PF loss)
  BDR_V3_C:       0.74  (PF improves with more trades)
  EMA9_V5_C:      0.79  (insensitive, keep tight)
  BS_STRUCT:       0.80  (small N, keep tight)

Comparison:
  Uniform 0.80: N=182, PF=2.20, +71.2R, DD=6.6R
  Uniform 0.77: N=224, PF=1.94, +73.6R, DD=9.4R
  Per-strategy:  N=201, PF=2.25, +81.6R, DD=6.6R  ← best

Implementation: ip_v2_threshold_by_strategy dict in config, checked
in replay gate against raw active_score (bypasses global active_passed).

commit.message:
fix(orchestration-scripts): add startup stale-requeue in chatgpt-driver.py

Root cause: when the research driver crashes/stops while items are
in_progress, those items remain stuck and block future dispatch.
On next startup, research-next returns nothing (no queued items)
and the driver idles indefinitely.

Fix: call research-requeue-stale(30) at driver startup so any items
stuck in_progress for >30 minutes are automatically requeued
(or failed permanently after 3 attempts), unblocking the pipeline.

Also documents the other root cause: nodriver package must be
installed (pip install -r .codex/scripts/requirements-research.txt)
before the driver can run at all.

fix: use Apify KV store for carousel image extraction

2. Apify KV store (query storeId from run data, filter keys by postId)
3. Direct TikTok page scrape (last resort, often blocked from Vercel IPs)

* fix(photos): P3→sRGB detection — ICC profile uses UTF-16, not ASCII

The ICC profile for Display P3 encodes the description as UTF-16

* fix(photos): encode photo clips as HEVC HLG to match video pipeline

Photo clips were H.264 SDR while iPhone videos are HEVC HLG 10-bit.
The assembly pipeline detected the mismatch and applied SDR→HDR zscale
conversion to the photos, producing red tint.

Fix: encode photo clips with matching HEVC HLG/BT.2020 metadata:
- macOS: hevc_videotoolbox -profile:v main10 -tag:v hvc1
- Linux: libx265 with HLG x265-params
- Color: bt2020nc/bt2020/arib-std-b67 (matches iPhone DV 8.4)
- Pixel format: p010le (10-bit, matches video clips)

No color conversion needed in assembly — photos and videos now speak
the same color language.

* fix(photos): add setparams filter to embed HLG/BT.2020 in photo clips

hevc_videotoolbox needs setparams in the filter chain to properly
embed color metadata in the output container. Without it, the color_transfer
and color_primaries fields are empty despite the encoder flags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(photos): zscale converts sRGB→HLG pixel data (not just metadata tags)

ROOT CAUSE: setparams only changes metadata, not pixel values. The
rendered frames are sRGB (gamma 2.2) but were tagged as HLG — when
decoded, the player applies inverse HLG transfer to sRGB data → red.

FIX: use zscale to ACTUALLY convert the transfer function:
  sRGB (iec61966-2-1) → HLG (arib-std-b67)
  BT.709 primaries → BT.2020 primaries
  BT.709 matrix → BT.2020NC matrix

The pixel values are now genuinely in HLG color space, matching the
iPhone video clips. No metadata lying, no assembly conversion needed.
